discussion_comments.comment_body: This won't work without subscribing to the atom
is this the same?  not sure what this.props vs this.props_ is here?
When trying out Preact, mobx was complaining about the way we accessed the props here. Copying the props to an observable solved it, not sure why exactly.

For now, if the user is not focused on the user input modal, they can still remove it or confirm it with escape or enter

I think this is the correct behavior, because we wouldn't want the keybindings to unregister and then have the user be able to access the layer below

I briefly thought of disabling rather than unregistering the keybindings if they're not in focus, and I can do that in the future if we want, but I think for now it makes sense not to disable them, because the modal is the only thing on the screen anyway

currently when you select the date picker for the first time, handleFocus gets called twice and so the keys get registered twice. I think that's because handleMouseDown calls handleFocus which calls event.target.select() which I think triggers handleFocus again ? 

Either way it's not a big deal and doesn't affect anything. I could in theory add an mobx observable here so that they don't register twice. like keybindsRegistered: OV<boolean> which then gets set to false when the keybinds get unregistered

I think this is worth doing because there's a log print that happens if a key bind gets registered twice
addressed - I implemented it as described

I added enter, escape and tab keybindings because I felt like they would be nice. enter toggles modal, escape closes modal, and tab does the same thing as arrow right, selects the next part of the date

some or key bindings here
I feel like we should make these either Ctrl or Cmd, rather than both

I think it should just be control?
Looking at this though I understand why or is used here since it's a really important command
